name: Hassenpfeffer#1
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissect and clean the rabbit.
2. Pour boiling water over it, scrape, rinse, and dry.
3. Combine vinegar, garlic, bay leaf, salt (optional), pepper, and 4 tablespoons of olive oil in a glass or pottery bowl.
4. Add the rabbit and marinate in the refrigerator for 48 hours, turning occasionally.
5. Drain the rabbit.
6. Cook the bacon in a Dutch oven until lightly browned.
7. Add the sliced onions and cook until golden.
8. Blend in flour and add the rabbit to the pot. Cook for 10 minutes, stirring several times.
9. Add the wine, bring to a boil, and stir in the chocolate. Cover and cook over low heat for 1-1/2 hours or until tender. Season with salt and pepper after 1 hour.
10. While cooking the rabbit, sauté the white onions in the remaining oil until golden.
11. Arrange the rabbit on a hot platter with the sautéed onions and bread around it.
12. Serve with noodles.
